meta.reason: Individuals with more than 3 prior offenses and at least one juvenile felony may have a higher risk of reoffending.
Individuals charged with a felony and having more than 1 prior offense may have a higher risk of reoffending.
A history of juvenile misdemeanors combined with multiple prior offenses could indicate a higher risk of reoffending.
Individuals charged with a felony and having more than 2 prior offenses are likely to reoffend.
Having a felony charge and more than 3 prior offenses suggests a pattern of serious criminal activity.
Younger individuals with a higher number of prior offenses may have a higher risk of reoffending.
Individuals with juvenile felony counts and a significant number of prior offenses may indicate persistent criminal behavior.
